title: Parameters Disclosure Envelope
description: Canonical wire shape of credentialSubject.action.parameters_disclosure when the field carries the asymmetric encryption envelope specified by ADR-0012 (v1 envelope). This schema describes the envelope's *content*; it is a sibling schema to agent-receipt.schema.json and is not embedded into the main receipt schema until the cross-SDK byte-identical interop tests land. Daemon-attested additive metadata (the existing peer.* / emitter.drop_count keys, per ADR-0010 and ADR-0012 Implementation status) and the legacy redacted-plaintext map (--parameter-disclosure flag) are NOT envelopes and are out of scope for this schema. Once a node opts in to envelope mode, parameters_disclosure MUST conform to this schema and the daemon's existing flat-map shape MUST NOT appear in the same field.

v.description: Envelope schema version. v1 pins the algorithm choice in `alg`, the single-shot HPKE single-recipient shape, and the field set defined here. Any field addition, removal, encoding change, or AEAD/KEM/KDF change is a v2. The version is a string (not an integer) so that hub re-verifiers can dispatch on the exact JCS-canonical bytes without ambiguity about JSON number encoding.

alg.description: HPKE ciphersuite tag in human-readable form, identifying the KEM / KDF / AEAD triple. v1 pins HPKE base mode with KEM=DHKEM(X25519, HKDF-SHA256) (0x0020), KDF=HKDF-SHA256 (0x0001), AEAD=AES-256-GCM (0x0002) per RFC 9180 §7. A string tag is used rather than the numeric ciphersuite triple so verifiers can dispatch by equality without parsing IANA registry IDs; the numeric triple is recorded in this description as the canonical cross-reference for implementers. Additional ciphersuites require a new ADR-0012 amendment and a v2 envelope.

recipients.description: Recipient descriptors. v1 is single-recipient (minItems=maxItems=1); the array shape is forward-compatible with the Phase C multi-recipient design (see ADR-0012 Phase C). When multi-recipient lands, this constraint relaxes to minItems: 1 with no upper bound and `ct` continues to be a single shared ciphertext under a per-envelope content-encryption key wrapped to each recipient's HPKE encapsulated key. Verifiers MUST treat the array as ordered: the index of a recipient's entry is its position in any future per-recipient wrapped-key array; canonical JSON preserves array order under RFC 8785.

ct.description: AEAD ciphertext including the 16-byte GCM authentication tag, encoded as unpadded base64url (RFC 4648 §5). The `pattern` enforces both the base64url alphabet (no `=` padding, no `+` or `/`) and decodable unpadded-base64url length: complete 4-char groups optionally followed by 2 or 3 trailing chars, which means `len % 4 != 1` — the one length residue that is invalid for base64url without padding. The `minLength` of 22 floors the value at the encoded size of the 16-byte GCM tag alone (the smallest possible AEAD output, which occurs only for an empty plaintext; v1 forbids empty plaintexts via the *shape invariants*, so real values will always exceed this). Plaintext is the RFC 8785 canonical JSON serialisation of the parameters object. The HPKE AAD is the empty string for v1 (single-shot, no out-of-band context binding beyond the receipt envelope itself, which already commits to `parameters_disclosure` by signature). Encoding follows the spec-wide ADR-0009 convention of unpadded base64url for binary fields (see spec §4 `proofValue`); standard base64 is not accepted.

kid.description: Recipient public-key identifier. SHOULD be either an ADR-0007 `did:key` DID URL with a fragment identifying the encryption verification method (e.g. `did:key:z6Mk...#enc-1`) or the `sha256:<hex>` fingerprint convention used elsewhere in the repo (per ADR-0015 "Fingerprint canonical form"). The kid is an index — it lets the forensic responder pick the right private key — and is not by itself authenticated; binding to the recipient public key happens through HPKE encapsulation.

enc.description: HPKE encapsulated key output (RFC 9180 §4.1, `enc`). For the DHKEM(X25519, HKDF-SHA256) KEM this is the 32-byte X25519 ephemeral public key, encoded as unpadded base64url. The `pattern` constrains the value to exactly 43 base64url-alphabet characters — the encoded length of 32 raw bytes with no padding (`ceil(32 * 4 / 3)` rounded up, minus 1 padding character that would normally complete the final 4-char block). The HPKE specification names this field `enc`; this schema uses the same name rather than an invented alternative (e.g. `wrapped_key`, `epk`, `encap`) so implementers can map directly to library APIs without translation. The original ADR-0012 sketch listed `encap` — that name is superseded here; see the ADR amendment for the rationale.
